message:
test(mcp-conformance): round fusion weights to 2 decimals in goldens (ft-5v7l0)

The wa_search golden froze raw f32→f64 casts:

    "lexical_weight":  0.30000001192092896
    "semantic_weight": 0.699999988079071

originating from effective_search_fusion_weights in
crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157. The operator contract is the
30/70 split; those trailing bits are representation noise that churns the
golden on any harmless f32 intermediate change (widening, different
codegen, stdlib bump) without catching a semantic regression.

Extend canonicalize() in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs to round
`lexical_weight` and `semantic_weight` to 2 decimals, matching the same
"freeze structural presence, not the incidental bits" pattern used for
elapsed_ms/captured_at/polls/score/semantic_score.

`(0.30000001192092896 * 100.0).round() / 100.0` round-trips through
serde_json::Number::from_f64 + ryu as exactly "0.3" in the serialized
golden; the companion edit pins the golden to 0.3 / 0.7. No new helper,
no test-behaviour change — semantic assertions in
assert_search_success_data at :446 already skip these fields.

Rounding instead of zeroing (the treatment applied to score /
semantic_score) preserves the operator-readable weight split in the
golden — a reviewer can still see "hybrid uses ~30/70" at a glance.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Zero ansi_byte_count after SIMD scan when flag is false; scan still runs to keep state.scan_state honest across chunks. Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.698143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.697874Z","close_reason":"Landed in commit 49937d56: storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing test pins the full StorageHandle reopen path. Asserts (1) fresh open with defer_fts_triggers=false installs all 3 output_segments_a[iud] triggers and synchronously indexes the first write, (2) reopen with defer_fts_triggers=false keeps all 3 triggers and synchronously indexes the second write via the trigger path. Combined with existing fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle (SQL-level true→false phase test) and fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip (5-iteration stress), the ft-ih4tm bidirectional contract is now pinned at both the connection and StorageHandle layers. Added Debug+Clone on StorageConfig for test ergonomics. All 3 tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:24:04.848456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6269x","title":"[reality-gap] ft snapshot restore Unix-only status is not disclosed in README session-persistence matrix","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:128 Session Persistence matrix row:

    | Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite |
    Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor`
    are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current
    WezTerm mux interop boundary. \`ft restart\` is currently
    Unix-only. |

Disclosed: `ft restart` Unix-only.
Undisclosed: `ft snapshot restore` has the *same* cfg gate.

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100` (ft restart — disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715` (ft snapshot restore — NOT disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft snapshot restore is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

Same gate, same failure mode. README line 128 specifically mentions
`ft restart` as the Unix-only surface but omits `ft snapshot restore`,
which is also mentioned in the *same* matrix row ("Snapshots, session
inspection, **restore**, and `ft session doctor` are implemented").

A Windows operator reading the matrix is told "restore is
implemented" (true, on Unix) without the cross-platform caveat.

# Failure shape

Operator on Windows runs `ft snapshot restore ...` → eprintln hits
→ exit 1. Workaround unknown.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:128 to say:

    Snapshots and session inspection are implemented cross-platform;
    `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are currently Unix-only.

One-line doc change. Alternative: fix the cfg gate. The session
persistence code under `#[cfg(unix)]` likely depends on real Unix
signals / fork semantics — porting is non-trivial. Docs-first is
the pragmatic path.

# Related

ft-2guux previously flagged G2 "`ft restart` Unix-only not in
support matrix" and was closed 2026-04-21 with a README.md:128
update. That update added disclosure for `ft restart` but missed
`ft snapshot restore` — this bead is the follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","owner":"pane3-readme-fix","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:55.113196Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.619147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208098Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","platform","reality-gap"]}
@@ -160,7 +160,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-fs42k","title":"connector_outbound_bridge: dedup uses event.timestamp_ms (parallel ft-dijpe miss)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1027\` passes
\`event.timestamp_ms\` (caller-supplied via the public
\`OutboundEvent::with_timestamp_ms\` builder, line 284) to
\`check_and_record\` as the dedup eviction clock — the exact shape that
ft-dijpe just fixed in the inbound bridge.

The ft-dijpe defect description even claimed *"Outbound bridge doesn't
have this shape — it uses its own \`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now()\`
for all internal state."* That claim is wrong. The outbound bridge's
constructor (line 245) does use \`SystemTime::now()\` to seed
\`timestamp_ms\` by default, but:

1. The public \`with_timestamp_ms\` builder (line 284) lets any caller
   override that default to an arbitrary value.
2. \`route_event\` line 1027 then passes that caller-controlled value
   directly to \`OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record\`, which calls
   \`evict_expired(now_ms)\` and pops anything whose
   \`now_ms - inserted_at_ms > ttl_ms\`.
3. Same replay-bypass attack as ft-dijpe: a caller submitting two
   copies of the same correlation_id with the second copy's
   \`timestamp_ms = ttl_ms + 1\` triggers eviction of the first entry
   and the replay is treated as new.

## Concrete failure path

1. Internal caller (workflow handler, retry loop, test harness) builds
   an \`OutboundEvent\` and calls \`with_timestamp_ms\` to override the
   default \`SystemTime::now()\` (legitimate reason: replaying historical
   events for backfill, fixture-driven tests, or a buggy clock-advance
   helper).
2. First dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=0\`.
3. Second dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1\`.
4. \`evict_expired\` sees \`now_ms=ttl+1 > inserted_at=0+ttl\` → pops X.
5. \`check_and_record\` finds cache empty, inserts a new X, returns
   \`true\` → second dispatch routes as if new. All downstream
   side-effects fire twice (slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR
   comment, credential broker call, etc.).

Trust model is weaker than inbound's — outbound events are
internally constructed rather than attacker-supplied — but the
defense-in-depth argument applies. A buggy internal caller that
mis-sets \`timestamp_ms\` should not silently bypass dedup, and
symmetry with the inbound bridge's hardening makes the codebase
easier to reason about.

## Severity rationale

- Same shape as ft-dijpe (silent dedup bypass), one severity level
  lower because the trust model is internal-only.
- Reachable today via any internal caller misusing
  \`with_timestamp_ms\`. Future caller bugs are likelier than
  attacker reach.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-dijpe fix: in \`route_event\` line 1027, compute
\`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_millis()\`
and pass that to \`check_and_record\` instead of \`event.timestamp_ms\`.
The \`event.timestamp_ms\` field stays useful as an analytics signal
(end-to-end source→sink latency, dispatch-history correlation) but
must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

## Regression test

\`bridge_dedup_ignores_event_timestamp_ms_for_eviction\`: dispatch X
with \`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=0\`, then dispatch X with
\`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2\`. Assert the second is
\`deduplicated=true\`. Pre-fix the second returns \`deduplicated=false\`
because evict_expired with the spoofed future-timestamp pops the live
entry.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs\` only.
The line-1080 \`let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms\` (used downstream for
\`created_at_ms\`, \`broker_checked_at_ms\`, \`dispatched_at_ms\`) is a
separate concern — those telemetry timestamps arguably should also
be SystemTime::now() but that's a wider change. This bead covers
ONLY the dedup eviction clock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:25:28.629996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.140282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.139925Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-ptjlp (same defect, same line, parallel filing race). Sibling agent already applied the SystemTime::now() fix in working tree at connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1037-1044 with their own ft-ptjlp tag in the inline comment. Closing this bead so ft-ptjlp owns the fix and the test work. Reviewed their fix — it's identical to what I would have written (SystemTime::now() → dedup_now_ms → check_and_record), correctly preserves event.timestamp_ms for downstream telemetry uses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fvt0b","title":"codec oversized-container regression overconstrains error wording","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:12.478186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.315201Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.314833Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 4d6c4abc; verified with codec --lib and conformance_pdu_wire","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-g3g1c","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml pins actions/attest to mutable @v4 tag","description":"release.yml:145 uses actions/attest@v4 — mutable major-version alias. Verified resolves to v4.1.0 today via GitHub releases API. actions/attest default mode (no sbomPath/predicate) IS SLSA provenance per src/detect.ts, so functionally correct. Concern: attestation action pinning by moving tag undermines its own supply-chain purpose. Fix: pin to 40-char SHA. Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:27.714567Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:06.046980Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:06.046712Z","close_reason":"Landed in eba16ce3: pinned actions/attest to immutable SHA 59d89421af93a897026c735860bf21b6eb4f7b26 (v4.1.0).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
-{"id":"ft-g3z31","title":"[review][LOW] ft-ojgmo lacks regression test for pre-cancel fast-path in EventSubscriber::recv_cx","description":"fa1fbffe added two cancel-detection paths to EventSubscriber::recv_cx at crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:789-817:

1. Pre-cancel fast-path at :793:
       if cx.is_cancel_requested() { return Err(RecvError::Cancelled); }
2. Mid-flight cancel watcher at :798-806 (select race with 50ms polling).

The new regression test `event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_mid_flight_cancel` at :1573-1601 exercises the MID-FLIGHT path only — cancel is signalled 100ms AFTER recv_cx is entered. The PRE-CANCEL fast-path (cx already cancelled when recv_cx is called) is untested at the EventSubscriber layer.

Evidence: search for calls to EventSubscriber::recv_cx with a pre-cancelled cx in the test suite — none exist. The runtime_compat.rs test at :6189 tests pre-cancel on the lower-level broadcast_recv_with_cx primitive, but not the EventSubscriber wrapper that is the public API.

Why it matters: the pre-cancel shortcut is one line, but it was the difference between a hot loop of polling wakeups and immediate return. A refactor that moves the cancel check inside the select! block (losing the shortcut) would regress the behavior silently — the mid-flight test would still pass because the 50ms poll would eventually fire.

Fix: add a sibling test:

    #[test]
    fn event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_pre_cancel_immediately() {
        run_async_test(async {
            let cx = crate::cx::Cx::for_testing();
            cx.cancel_with(crate::outcome::CancelKind::User, Some("pre-cancel"));
            let bus = EventBus::new(8);
            let mut sub = bus.subscribe();
            let started = Instant::now();
            let result = sub.recv_cx(&cx).await;
            assert!(matches!(result, Err(RecvError::Cancelled)));
            assert!(started.elapsed() < Duration::from_millis(5),
                "pre-cancel must return immediately, took {:?}", started.elapsed());
        });
    }

A <5ms assertion distinguishes the shortcut path from the 50ms poll path.

Severity: LOW — not a correctness bug, just a coverage gap. Discovered during reviewer audit round 11, 2026-04-23 while verifying fa1fbffe.

Tag: review, events, cancellation, test-coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:57.398125Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:18:12.171325Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
+{"id":"ft-g3z31","title":"[review][LOW] ft-ojgmo lacks regression test for pre-cancel fast-path in EventSubscriber::recv_cx","description":"fa1fbffe added two cancel-detection paths to EventSubscriber::recv_cx at crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:789-817:

1. Pre-cancel fast-path at :793:
       if cx.is_cancel_requested() { return Err(RecvError::Cancelled); }
2. Mid-flight cancel watcher at :798-806 (select race with 50ms polling).

The new regression test `event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_mid_flight_cancel` at :1573-1601 exercises the MID-FLIGHT path only — cancel is signalled 100ms AFTER recv_cx is entered. The PRE-CANCEL fast-path (cx already cancelled when recv_cx is called) is untested at the EventSubscriber layer.

Evidence: search for calls to EventSubscriber::recv_cx with a pre-cancelled cx in the test suite — none exist. The runtime_compat.rs test at :6189 tests pre-cancel on the lower-level broadcast_recv_with_cx primitive, but not the EventSubscriber wrapper that is the public API.

Why it matters: the pre-cancel shortcut is one line, but it was the difference between a hot loop of polling wakeups and immediate return. A refactor that moves the cancel check inside the select! block (losing the shortcut) would regress the behavior silently — the mid-flight test would still pass because the 50ms poll would eventually fire.

Fix: add a sibling test:

    #[test]
    fn event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_pre_cancel_immediately() {
        run_async_test(async {
            let cx = crate::cx::Cx::for_testing();
            cx.cancel_with(crate::outcome::CancelKind::User, Some("pre-cancel"));
            let bus = EventBus::new(8);
            let mut sub = bus.subscribe();
            let started = Instant::now();
            let result = sub.recv_cx(&cx).await;
            assert!(matches!(result, Err(RecvError::Cancelled)));
            assert!(started.elapsed() < Duration::from_millis(5),
                "pre-cancel must return immediately, took {:?}", started.elapsed());
        });
    }

A <5ms assertion distinguishes the shortcut path from the 50ms poll path.

Severity: LOW — not a correctness bug, just a coverage gap. Discovered during reviewer audit round 11, 2026-04-23 while verifying fa1fbffe.

Tag: review, events, cancellation, test-coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:57.398125Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.679034Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.678785Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 8ae03256: add EventSubscriber::recv_cx pre-cancel regression test that asserts immediate Err(Cancelled) before any emission.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
